q: Fluid is a substance that offers no permanent resistance to deforming forces.
Fluid dynamics concentrates on fluids at rest, while fluid statics concerns fluids in motion.
The study of the flow of liquid is called hydrodynamics.
Liquid is compressible meaning density of liquid is constant for any pressure provided the temperature is kept constant.
In laminar flow, streamlines do not cross each other and every fluid particle arriving at a given point has the same velocity.
In turbulent flow, the speed of the fluid at a point is constant in both magnitude and direction.
Ideal laminar fluid flow considers the fluid to be incompressible.
Internal friction in fluids is called viscosity and ideal fluids have high viscosity.
Motion of fluids can be classified by two types, laminar flow and turbulent flow.
The volume of a gas depends on pressure and temperature obeying Newton's laws of motion.
If fluid particles are moving steady in smooth paths in layers, with each layer moving smoothly past the adjacent layers with no mixing; such a flow is called laminar flow.
A storm is an example of laminar flow while a sea breeze is a turbulent flow.
Streamline is the path or flow of the fluid particles in the flow of fluids.
Fluid statics is the study of liquid in motion.
The volume of a liquid does not depend on pressure if the temperature is constant.
In laminar flow, the path of the fluid particles are irregular and their direction is always changing or whirling.
